title: Creating Custom email template to send when status Changes
excerpt: Can someone please assist we are looking to send an automatic personalised email to our clients when an order is dispatched (status changes to something) - however I would like this email to be more graphically pleasing …

posts.author: Sam1 (Sam)
posts.cookedHtml:
Can someone please assist we are looking to send an automatic personalised email to our clients when an order is dispatched (status changes to something) - however I would like this email to be more graphically pleasing than the standard monday.com email and potentially sent from an outlook account

So my question is can I create a custom colour/font/text in this Automation, we want to include the information on the board like consignment note and courier so would be great if it was created in Monday but I would like to be able to make the look of the email fit with our branding ?

posts.author: bradley (Bradley Vanderhorst)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Sam1 - welcome to the monday.com community an extension of the monday.com family!

At this time the Email integration does not support HTML coding. We are hoping to add that function in the future!

Stay tuned.

posts.author: KirstyCoaching (Kirsty McWilliam)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there, has there been an update on this? Even just the ability to change the font?
Thanks, Kirsty

posts.author: bradley (Bradley Vanderhorst)
posts.cookedHtml: No update - perhaps by the end of year we’ll have that support.

posts.author: AndyClayton (Andy Clayton)
posts.cookedHtml: I have seen so many threads on this topic. It’s one of the really big missing pieces of making monday.com truly a system for managing the whole business. Any updates on when this will happen? If we could really use monday.com to send personalised, automated emails (with attachments, graphics, dropdowns for recipients…), to customers, to prospects, to team members, beyond just the simple ‘notify’ notes that currently exist, it would make the system truly powerful.

posts.author: WhitneyWells (Whitney Wells)
posts.cookedHtml: @monday-team Any update on this!!! This would be HUGE for our workflow.

posts.author: Mitchkv (Mitchell Vernon)
posts.cookedHtml: @monday-team another one for an update on this? I’ve just got monday and love the features but this is a must have for me unfortunately.

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Mitchkv @WhitneyWells @AndyClayton

While still not natively supported within Monday, it can be setup with ease. You have a couple of options:

  1. Integration through Integromat or Zapier. With this option you get the most flexibility but probably the most setup required. But with that, you get the ability to control the process from start to finish. This is the method I currently use to send all sorts of emails based off automations. If you have any questions on how this would work or look like, feel free to message me directly.

  2. Apps - I’ve seen in another thread an app created by @upitchika if you want to reach out to him directly. That’s all I know on that option.

  3. CommuniHub - This is a recent addition that allows you to send emails from the Update section of an item. Worth checking out although I’m not sure it will be the solution you are looking for.

posts.author: Chelsea (Chelsea Parrott)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Sam1

While it does not currently have a status trigger, I think the native CommuniHub App may be a solution for you. You can create templates and email directly from an update on monday.com

The email sent from this app looks more like a typical outlook or gmail email, rather than the monday notification email that you may be used to.

Check it out here!:

Support

Emails & Activities on monday.com

8 min read Feature   With the Emails & Activities app, your team can easily manage client or customer relationships in a centralized way, meaning that your team will spend less time trac...

posts.author: lingalligatorpools (Ling)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, we just recently integrated CommuniHub to our Monday account in hopes of removing the need to be shifting from application to application (we use Outlook for emails). We love the fact you can send emails straight from Monday, however, when trying to create email templates, we can’t find a way to use our HTML templates. Not sure if there is a way to get this done… any suggestions?

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
@lingalligatorpools

I’m not sure the Communihub App supports importing your own HTML templates yet. Not sure if they plan on that or what an ETA would look like.

In the meantime, going the Integromat/Zapier route would allow full HTML customization though.

posts.author: lingalligatorpools (Ling)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Andrew, we’ll check that out. It’d be great if CommuniHub added it, this way we can keep the app integrations at a minimum.

posts.author: dylandunaway (Dylan Dunaway)
posts.cookedHtml:
@monday-team CommuniHub doesn’t offer the HTML sending that I’m looking for and Zapier doesn’t recognize the columns properly to allow for that integration.

Please update us when we may expect the update in integrations.

posts.author: DarrenVenter (Darren)
posts.cookedHtml: Has anyone seen an update to this question yet?

posts.cookedHtml: Have there been any developments with this at all? Looks to be a fairly common problem people are running into, including myself.

posts.author: bask (Nick_Bask)

It would be great to get an update about this. Customised HTLM emails would be a great way for us to engage with our clients directly from our Monday project boards. The ability to choose different templates based on the status change would be great too.

Thanks
N

posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this feature?

posts.author: juztn24 (JC)
posts.cookedHtml: This discussion has been going on for a couple of years now. @monday-team @monday-moderators Do you have an update as of Oct 2022 on where customized HTML email stands?

posts.cookedHtml: The most important thing why I’ve chosen Monday is the email templates. And now I sadly recognized that okay, I can make templates, but can’t automate them. I would pay the twice of our plan if it will work.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey all! We hear you on this, and have followed up internally with our developers as to where customised HTML email stands at this current time. We will follow up here once we hear back with more information. Thanks for your patience!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: https://monday.slack.com/archives/C010R8M0G4A/p1666761896920919

posts.author: 1cameron (Cameron)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Bianca! Do you have any updates on this?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for your patience with me on this! I’ve heard back from one of our product leads who has confirmed that this is a feature they’re considering prioritising for the next Q I understand this isn’t a huge development but be assured that it is on their radar!

posts.author: GreenRushByDesign (Jamila)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on adding a custom email template to automation

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @GreenRushByDesign!

As of now, I do not have a concrete update to provide however I do encourage you all to cast your vote on the feature request here as every quarter we take the features with the top votes to our development team for a response and status update. As you can see this feature is currently under consideration, and we will be providing an update on any new developments once we receive an update from our product team

posts.author: Maryam (Maryam )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Andrew,

I saw a thread where you talked about automatically sending templated emails using Zapier. Can you please give me some more info on it?

I am looking for a way to save templated emails and auto send them when a date column arrives. The important thing is I am using some of the column’s info on the emails and I’m not sure how to keep those info when saving a template somewhere else.

Thank you!

posts.author: kevin_vaghasiya (kevin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @sam1
You can create email templates easily with the EasyMails app. Just drag and drop to design them the way you want, matching your brand. It works with Gmail and Outlook, allowing you to send emails from your account.

EasyMails_gallary-041920×960 198 KB

posts.author: dharmik (Dharmik Jagodana)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you want to add images that visually represent the order status based on real-time order updates, you can use the imejis.io app. This app allows you to create dynamic images using data from your Monday board. Once the image is generated, you can include it in your HTML emails, providing a clear and engaging way to show order tracking updates to your customers. This makes it easier for them to understand the status of their order at a glance.

Example image
